resources.string: Choose a delivery master, social render, audio mix, or clean still with confidence.
Start with a platform-ready preset, then fine-tune the details if this render needs something custom.
Skip video rendering and export a single audio master.
%1$d%% of the timeline can stream through. %2$d ranges copy directly; %3$d need re-encode.
Adjust the specs NovaCut will use for this export.
Constrain output to fit platform upload limits. Auto-picks the bitrate.
Heads up: estimated render time is %1$s. Exports run in the background, but plug in to avoid battery drain.
Estimated output is over 1 GB. Most share targets reject files this large — consider a Target File Size preset.
AV1 is slow on most Android devices. If file size isn\'t critical, HEVC encodes much faster.
Burn a brand image into every frame of the exported video.
Grid of clip thumbnails as a single PNG — great for review decks and teasers.
Turn on alternate deliverables like subtitles, stems, GIFs, or still captures.
Save subtitles alongside the main export in your chosen format.
Export separate audio files for each track in the timeline.
Include chapter markers in supported timeline exports.
Hand the edit off to another timeline tool without committing to a media render.
Create a lightweight looping GIF instead of a video file.
Export the current frame as a still image.
Double-check the destination, then render the version you need.
This frees about 75 MB and disables new transcription until you download Whisper again. Existing captions and edits stay in the project.
This removes the local background-segmentation file and disables AI matte quality until you download it again. Existing edits stay intact.
Whisper model removed. You can download it again any time.
Segmentation model removed. You can download it again any time.
NovaCut couldn\'t remove that model. Try again from AI Tools.
These assists can run immediately with the current timeline selection and downloaded models.
Select a clip on the timeline to unlock the tools below.
Tap to stage or run this assist.
Uses AI matte when installed, fallback otherwise.
Uses OpenCV when available, fallback otherwise.
Select a clip to unlock this workflow.
AI green screen with RVM matting
Analyzes audio to detect noise type (hiss, hum, broadband) and automatically applies the best DSP filters.
Adjust audio levels to a target loudness standard
Tune export defaults, timeline behavior, AI models, and editing comfort in one place.
Choose the defaults NovaCut reaches for when you start a new export.
Used for fresh renders until you override it.
Sets the playback cadence for new deliveries.
Applied when you start a new project or template.
Pick the delivery format NovaCut suggests first.
Keep navigation, snapping, and playback feeling crisp.
Keep a fresh recovery copy while you edit.
Controls how lightweight generated proxy media should be.
Use lighter preview media when timelines start to feel heavy.
Manage downloaded AI models for on-device processing.
Replay the first-run walkthrough whenever you need a refresher.
Show the guided editor walkthrough the next time NovaCut opens.
Build and engine details for troubleshooting and support.
Move the playhead or jump back to visible content.
NovaCut couldn\'t load this source for live preview.
Shape overlays that feel designed, timed, and intentional.
Microphone permission is needed to record voiceovers.
Balance the clip mix, fades, and voiceover without leaving the edit.
Record narration directly over the cut.
Select a timeline clip to reveal waveform, fades, level control, and voiceover options here.
Waveform preview will appear once NovaCut has decoded this clip’s audio.
Record narration directly over the cut without leaving the audio workspace.
Shape primary tone, curves, qualifiers, and LUTs while preview stays in motion.
Lift shapes the shadows, gamma handles the mids, and gain pushes the highlights.
Offset adds or subtracts overall channel energy before the rest of the grade.
Add points directly on the graph to remap tonal response for each channel.
Set a clean playback rate for the whole clip, then fine-tune with the logarithmic slider.
Stage ramps and peaks across the clip, then reshape them directly on the graph.
Double-tap the graph to add a point, then drag existing points to sculpt the timing.
Use the quick speeds for common retimes, then refine the result with the fine control slider.
No masks. Tap + to add one.
Shape attention, blur, and reveal areas directly over the frame without leaving the cut.
Create geometric or freehand masks, then tune feather, opacity, inversion, and motion behavior from one place.
Select a mask to refine it, or add another shape for layered reveals and local adjustments.
Flip the inside and outside of the mask.
Keep the mask attached to tracked movement when supported by the shot.
Add a shape above or tap an existing mask to adjust feather, opacity, inversion, and motion behavior.
Add your own images as stickers
No exports queued. Tap + to add.
Build a stack of exports for different platforms and let NovaCut run them back-to-back.
Tap any preset to add it to the queue, then keep stacking utility exports for audio-only or stems if you need them.
Review the stack, remove anything you no longer need, and launch the batch when it looks right.
Leave the add panel open and tap the targets you want to stack up.
NovaCut will export each item in order and keep its status here.
Bring in footage, photos, and audio or capture something new without leaving the edit.
Import the exact source type you need and keep picked media stable for editing.
Add timeline-ready clips from your gallery or file browser.
Add stills, title graphics, overlays, or visual references.
Add music, voiceover, ambience, or sound effects.
Select multiple photos or videos in one pass.
Record a clip without leaving NovaCut. Camera permission is only requested when you start recording.
Camera permission is needed to record video.
NovaCut couldn\'t keep that media available locally. Try choosing it again.
Some media couldn\'t be kept locally. The successful imports were added.
Keeping selected files available in this edit.
Creating a stable local copy for editing.
Keeping this still available in the project.
Moving the recorded clip into your project media.
That file isn\'t audio. Pick a .mp3, .m4a, .wav, .ogg, or .flac.
Create sharper edits with less friction.
Start from a structured template, resume a recent cut, or bring in a saved setup.
Start with a guided template or open a clean timeline and build from scratch.
Try another keyword or clear the filter to bring every project back.
Tap + to create your first project
Preparing the timeline, tracks, and autosave state.
Copying \"%1$s\" with its saved edit state.
Copying, validating, and opening the source as a new edit.
Checking the file and adding it to saved templates.
Creating a new edit from the saved structure.
NovaCut couldn\'t import that video. Choose a readable video file and try again.
Choose a template to get started
Use a built-in workflow, reopen one of your saved templates, or import a setup from storage.
Load a template file and continue editing with the same layout, aspect ratio, and tracks.
Pick a starting layout tuned for the kind of project you are making.
Saved setups stay here so you can reuse your favorite structure in new edits.
Save a project as a template to keep favorite track layouts, aspect ratios, and starter overlays ready.
Talk to camera with B-roll cutaways
Bring in clips, photos, or audio to start shaping the story on the timeline.
Tap the + button or use the menu to add media
Select a clip to use Effects
Select a clip to color grade
Select a clip for blend mode
Select a clip for chroma key
Tap an overlay to refine its copy or timing.
Layer tasteful looks, filters, and stylized treatments.
Dial the effect in, then keep only what helps the shot.
Control tempo, punch, and reverse playback with precision.
The clip is currently playing backward.
Play the clip in its natural direction.
Refine framing, scale, rotation, and overall presence.
Check position, scale, rotation, and opacity before you fine-tune the shot.
Adjust where the clip sits in frame and how much room it takes up.
Dial in rotation and opacity so layered shots feel intentional.
Pick the canvas that matches where this cut will live.
Choose a canvas built for where this edit will be published.
Blend between shots with transitions that feel intentional.
Choose a transition style and tune its timing to shape how shots hand off.
Start with a clean move, then slow it down only as much as the cut needs.
Short transitions keep the edit moving. Longer ones feel more stylized and deliberate.
Review the recent edit stack and jump back to a known-good state without repeated undo taps.
No undoable actions have been recorded in this session yet.
The highlighted row is the current state. Tap any earlier step to roll the timeline back in one move.
No edits to roll back yet
As soon as you trim, move, or adjust something on the timeline, NovaCut will keep those steps here so you can restore an earlier state confidently.
Newer steps stay visible for context. Use Redo to move forward again.
Delete this clip? This action can be undone.
Select a clip to edit audio
Clearer spacing for explainers and tutorials.
Slower cinematic read with more weight.
Sign in with Google to back up your projects
Save a snapshot before a risky trim, grade, or timing pass so you can jump back without losing momentum.
Delete “%1$s” from version history? This cannot be undone.
No chapters. Tap + to add at playhead.
No favorites yet. Tap the star on any effect.
Automatically detect and remove filler words (um, uh, like, you know) and silent gaps from your clip.
Spot filler words and dead air, then clean the cut in one pass before you polish pacing by hand.
NovaCut looks for filler speech and silent gaps so you can remove the soft spots before the final trim pass.
Suggestions stay focused on filler phrases and dead air so the edit stays tight without flattening intentional pauses.
Run analysis first. If the clip is already tight, NovaCut will leave the pacing untouched.
Review the suggested filler words and silent gaps, then remove them in one pass to tighten the performance.
NovaCut is scanning the selected clip for filler speech and dead air so cleanup suggestions feel deliberate instead of aggressive.
Start with a quick scan and NovaCut will surface the spots most likely to benefit from cleanup.
Scan the clip first to unlock one-tap cleanup.
AI analyzes your clips for quality, motion, and faces, then creates a highlight reel with the best moments.
Choose how the selected clip fuses with the layers under it, from subtle darkening to full stylized composites.
See which sections can stream through untouched and which shots still need a full render pass.
Add clips to the timeline and NovaCut will map which ranges can copy straight through and which still need rendering.
Every analyzed segment can stream through untouched, so this export should finish closer to a file copy than a full render.
Most of the timeline can copy straight through, with encode time focused only on the ranges you changed.
Every analyzed segment needs processing, so expect this export to behave like a traditional full render.
Each block shows whether NovaCut can copy that range directly or has to render it again.
Once clips are on the timeline, NovaCut will break the sequence into stream-copy and re-encode ranges here.
Generate a lightweight check render first, or jump straight into the full export flow with the current settings.
Add at least one clip to unlock preview and export actions.
Stream copied directly with no image-processing work.
Review linked source files, spot missing footage, and keep the timeline stack lean before export.
Check what is linked, what is offline, and whether any empty tracks can be trimmed out of the stack.
NovaCut is checking every source on the timeline so missing footage and cleanup opportunities are surfaced before export.
Resolve missing source files before export so previews, renders, and relink decisions stay predictable.
Your source media is available on this device, so preview, export, and handoff workflows should stay reliable.
Jump straight to the first use of a clip or review missing files before they turn into export surprises.
Imported clips, photos, and audio will appear here as soon as something lands on the timeline.
No empty non-default tracks are hanging around right now.
NovaCut found empty non-default tracks that can be removed to tighten the edit stack.
Mark selected clips for organization without changing the edit.
Export your project as a backup archive to Downloads/NovaCut/. Import to restore on any device.
Package the project into a portable archive or restore a previous backup without leaving the edit.
Create a project archive before risky changes so you have a clean rollback point that travels with the edit.
NovaCut is packaging the timeline, linked media references, and current project state into a portable archive.
NovaCut is unpacking the archive, reconnecting bundled media, and rebuilding the timeline state.
Keep a recent backup on hand before major revisions, device transfers, or client review notes.
Exports land in Downloads/NovaCut so they are easy to share, move to another device, or keep as rollback points before major edits.
Each archive keeps the edit portable without baking in a render, so restores stay fast and faithful to the current project state.
Create a fresh archive before risky changes, or import one to restore the whole project on this device.
Backup actions unlock again as soon as the current archive operation finishes.
This imports the selected backup into the open project and replaces the current timeline, overlays, markers, drawings, beats, and transcript. NovaCut saves an undo point before restoring.
Add music to the audio track for beat-synced cuts
Save, swap, and reuse effect chains so your look stays consistent across clips and projects.
Save, share, and reuse effect chains across clips and projects.
Capture the selected clip effect stack as a reusable chain.
Select a clip to capture its current chain.
Apply the buffered chain to the selected clip in one move.
Select a clip before you paste the buffered chain.
Copy a chain first so there is something ready to paste.
Package the selected clip look into an .ncfx preset you can share.
Select a clip before exporting its look as a preset.
Bring in a saved preset chain and keep the grade or treatment consistent.
Select a clip to copy, paste, or export its effects.
Search notes, filter by color, and jump straight to the moments that matter in the cut.
No timeline markers yet. Add markers while reviewing timing, notes, or audio beats.
Use search and color filters to tighten review passes without scrubbing the whole timeline.
Try a broader search or clear the current color filter to bring markers back into view.
Save your project state to roll back later
Create restore points before experimenting, then roll the timeline back without losing your place.
Name this restore point so you can jump back before a risky edit or export pass.
The latest snapshot was saved %1$s. Keep a clean trail before major timing or grading changes.
Snapshots are lightweight checkpoints for your edit state before you commit to bigger decisions.
Restore a checkpoint to revisit a previous direction, or delete older versions once the edit path feels settled.
Check overall channel balance and clipped highs or lows at a glance.
Compare luma and channel distribution across the frame while you grade.
See hue and saturation drift so skin tones and brand colors stay intentional.
Pause on a visible frame or scrub the timeline so NovaCut can analyze the current image.
NovaCut is sampling the current image for the selected scope.
Start from polished lower thirds, title cards, and CTAs instead of styling every text layer by hand.
Static presets are great for fast lower thirds and branded text. Animated presets help build more theatrical intros, callouts, and end cards.
These presets apply layered typography instantly, so you can keep moving and only fine-tune the moments that matter.
These templates lean into reveals, countdowns, and punchy motion. They are ideal when the title card needs to feel like part of the edit.
No templates in this view yet
Switch modes or choose a different category to keep browsing preset text looks.
Speaker IDs, headlines, and lower-third overlays.
Full-frame openers and cinematic title moments.
Outro cards, thank-you screens, and sign-offs.
Conversion-focused prompts for product and creator edits.
Handles, social tags, and creator identity treatments.
Clean typography for chapter markers and quotes.
Stage animation points, compare active curves, and shape how a clip moves over time.
Double-tap the curve view to add a keyframe on the first active property, or pick an existing diamond to refine its motion.
%1$s is selected. Drag it in the curve view or change how it blends into the next move below.
Turn on at least one property to start plotting motion curves and keyframes.
Turn on the curves you want to compare, then double-tap the graph to add new points at the playhead area.
Turn on a property first so the graph knows which curve to add new keyframes to.
Tap a diamond to select it, drag to reposition it, or double-tap the graph to add a point on the first active property.
Pick a property above to light up its motion curve and make the graph interactive.
This clip does not have a usable duration yet, so the curve view cannot be drawn.
Fine-tune this point’s timing, value, and interpolation without losing the bigger curve context.
Tap a keyframe diamond to inspect it here, or double-tap the graph to add a new point on the active property.
Turn hooks, explainers, and voiceover notes into a timeline-ready read without leaving the edit.
TTS not available on this device
Draft narration, read a call to action aloud, or rough in guide VO before you record the final take.
Each style shifts cadence and pitch so you can audition different delivery moods quickly.
Preview the pacing first, then generate a fresh voiceover clip for the timeline.
Add a line or two before previewing or generating speech.
Higher-fidelity offline voices are planned for a future update.
Tap the + button to create your first video project.
NovaCut couldn\'t create that project right now. Check available storage and try again.
NovaCut couldn\'t duplicate that project right now.
NovaCut couldn\'t copy that video into local storage.
Choose how much control and guidance the editor surfaces by default.
Display audio waveforms on timeline clips
Snap clip edges to detected beat markers
Snap clip edges to timeline markers
Choose how roomy new tracks should feel.
Pick the editing depth NovaCut opens with.
Vibrate on timeline snap and clip selection
Show confirmation dialog before deleting clips
Reserve more memory for faster thumbnail scrubbing.
Set the quality balance for quick one-tap exports.
Reset the first-run tutorial? You will see the tutorial again next time you open the editor.
Chapters are embedded in MP4 exports for YouTube navigation
Open the Add Media menu to import videos, photos, or audio from your device. You can also capture new footage with your camera.
Drag, trim, and arrange your clips on the multi-track timeline. Pinch to zoom and swipe to scroll through your project.
Apply effects, transitions, text overlays, and AI-powered tools from the toolbar. Tap a clip to see editing options.
When you\'re ready, tap the export button to render your video. Choose from platform presets for YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and more.
Force a transparent WebM render for overlays, stingers, or alpha assets.
This source file is missing on this device. Relink it before export so previews and renders do not fall back to missing media.
Capture timestamps, review notes, and cut ideas. Notes stay with this project and save automatically.
Add edit notes, feedback, timestamps, or ideas…
NovaCut restored this project from the last auto-save. Keep the recovered changes, or discard them and reload the saved version?
Your video has been exported successfully
No media yet. Add media above to build the project library.
No clips on this track yet
Trim mode: drag edges to resize; drag the clip body to slip media.
Pick a more expressive caption look, from understated subtitles to bold karaoke moments.
These templates update the selected clip’s captions in one shot, so it is easy to move from utility subtitles to a branded treatment.
Word-by-word, bounce, and sing-along styles built to feel animated and rhythmic.
Classic subtitle, lower-third, neon, and bold center treatments for more intentional framing.
Timeline overview — tap or drag to seek
